scope: M6 follow-on: word-structured packed-Q4_K scheduler GEMV (Q4K_GEMV_SCHEDULER=2, extra/qk_q4k_scheduler_gemv.py) vs fp16-logical scheduler GEMV (=1) vs owned warp kernel. FFN gate/up. Research-only; no default change.

root_cause_debug.load_pattern: alu0 = gidx0*55296 + lidx0*1728 -> adjacent lanes read words 1728 apart (3 rows x 576 words/row) = UNCOALESCED; K never grouped onto lanes
root_cause_debug.owned_contrast: one workgroup/row, lanes = word-col (lane4=pos//4) + 4-way block-group-K -> 8 adjacent lanes read 8 adjacent words (coalesced)

conclusion: A pure-scheduler Q4_K GEMV cannot match owned: fp16-logical ~2x off, word-structured packed ~4.6x off (WORSE -- restructuring the dequant expression produced strided uncoalesced word loads). The thread-map is the heuristic's choice, not the expression's; the lowerer does not synthesize coalesced packed-word loads from the gather. The owned kernel's coalesced packed-word load + per-row-workgroup K-split thread-map is a CUSTOM/codegen capability, NOT reachable by restructuring the dequant or by MV opts. Closes 'can a pure scheduler GEMV match owned' = NO, gap attributed.
missing_search_space_primitive: codegen capability: emit a coalesced packed-word (uint32) load with a custom per-row-workgroup + word-col-lane thread-map (what the owned kernel hand-codes via CUSTOM)
next: either a CUSTOM/codegen capability for coalesced packed-word loads + custom thread-map (deep), or pivot to the v_dot2 renderer lowering (attention tile gap). Cross-lane (M5) remains the only in-model-proven scheduler GEMV primitive but is low-leverage.
